rows.notes: Clear, centered year at top of page; no monetary amounts present.

rows.description: Computus Dni. Rectoris J. C. Richards
rows.notes: Title line clearly legible though the Latin abbreviation Dni. is slightly stylised; no amounts.
Date line clear and centred; no monetary values.
Phrase legible though small flourish on 'revolutionum'; no amounts.
Header is in ornate script; I read it as 'Arrearagia Recepta' (some letters ambiguous - e.g. rr/rt shapes) but clearly a section heading with no amounts.
Clear English heading under the section title; no amounts.
Amount '60' clearly aligned in the pounds column; shillings and pence columns empty. Place name and 'Rectory 1 yr' clear.
Amount '10' clearly in pounds column. 'do' (ditto) and '1/2 yr' legible.
Numeric grouping '1 17 6' aligns to £/s/d columns and all three values are clearly formed. 'Capons & Brown' reading is clear.
Amount reads as '9 6 8' (pounds, shillings, pence) with digits clearly in their columns. The place name 'Lad Lane' is slightly stylised but readable; surname 'Norris' clear.
Amount '6 13 4' is clearly aligned. The place name has an uncertain spelling (reads as 'Dunnithorne' / 'Dunithorne'); chosen the closest visual reading.
Amount '1 13 4' is clear. 'Rickford' reading is fairly legible in the script.
Amount '6 13 4' aligns well. Place name looks like 'Medenham' though older spelling could vary; recorded exactly as it appears.

Amount appears as '1 19 1/2' (pounds, shillings, pence = 1 and a half). The place name is uncertain in the handwriting (read as 'Little New Bucks' from the script); fraction interpreted from a small '1/2' after the pence figure.
Amount '17 6' (pounds and shillings) is legible; the place name reads like 'Lymath' but could be slightly different (handwriting ambiguity). No pence shown.
Amount appears as a single '7' in the pounds column. The name reads 'Lennard' though script could allow alternate spellings; shillings and pence blank.
Amount '10' clear in pounds column. 'Bushley Tenements' interpretation is legible.
Section header clearly written; no amounts on this header line.
Numeric '11 2 9' is well aligned and legible. 'Kidlington Rectory 1/2 yr' reading clear.
Amount '28' clearly in pounds column. 'L Wittenham' (Long Wittenham abbreviation) is readable.
Amount '4 4' (pounds and shillings) is legible; pence column blank. 'Do Vicarage' (ditto) clear.
Amount '11' in pounds column clear. Place name reads 'Yarnton' distinctly.
Clear section heading; no amounts present on the line.
Amount '84 0 6' aligns clearly in £/s/d columns. Text 'Kidlington Augmentation 1/2 yr' is legible.
Amount '34 8 4' clear and passes currency validation. 'Do Mills 2 yrs' (ditto Mills) readable.
Large amount '107 14 3' is well aligned and clear. Place and 'Rectory 1 yr' legible.

rows.description: Sth Newington Rectory 1 yr last Day 1838
rows.notes: Amount '181 19 9' is clear. The place line includes 'Newington Rectory' and a trailing note 'last Day 1838' — the abbreviation for the first word (Sth/St.) is somewhat stylised; transcribed as seen.
Amount reads as '2 14 8 1/4' with a small '1/4' after the pence column. Alignment indicates pounds=2, shillings=14, pence=8 and a farthing (0.25). The leading 'Do' is ditto from previous place.
Amount appears as '239 7 5 1/4' (pounds, shillings, pence and a farthing). 'Land Tax deducted' note in parentheses is legible. Fraction 1/4 is faint but visible.

Amount '2 10 10 1/2' visible; half-penny indicated by '1/2' after pence. Place name 'Tintinhull - Beaton' and date clause 'to Michl 1838' read from abbreviated script; moderate uncertainty in spelling.

Amount appears as '15 13 3 3/4' (three farthings = 0.75) — the '3/4' fraction is visible though cramped. Description is abbreviated ('Penny Do Do') and somewhat unclear in the handwriting; transcribed as it appears.

Final total at bottom with currency symbol. Reads as '£834 7 3 3/4' — pounds and shillings are clear; pence shows 3 with three-quarters (three farthings) indicated. Alignment consistent with total line; some minor smudging around fraction but still legible.
